Terminator: Genisys (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $89,760,956Details
International Box Office $342,389,938
Worldwide Box Office $432,150,894
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $9,102,495
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $17,622,436
Total $26,724,931
Further financial details...

Synopsis

When John Connor, leader of the human resistance, sends Sgt. Kyle Reese back to 1984 to protect Sarah Connor and safeguard the future, an unexpected turn of events creates a fractured timeline. Now, Sgt. Reese finds himself in a new and unfamiliar version of the past, where he is faced with unlikely allies, including the Guardian, dangerous new enemies, and an unexpected new mission: To reset the future…

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$27,018,486 (30.1% of total gross)
Legs:3.32 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:20.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$155,000,000 (worldwide box office is 2.8 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,758 opening theaters/3,783 max. theaters, 3.7 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $127,347,688

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: July 1st, 2015 (Wide) by Paramount Pictures
July 1st, 2015 (IMAX) by Paramount Pictures
International Releases: June 24th, 2015 (Wide) (Indonesia)
June 25th, 2015 (Wide) (Sweden)
June 25th, 2015 (Wide) (Greece)
June 26th, 2015 (Wide) (Norway)
June 26th, 2015 (Wide) (Singapore)
Video Release: October 20th, 2015 by Paramount Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense sequences of sci -fi violence and gunplay throughout, partial nudity and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2378, 6/17/2015)
Running Time: 126 minutes
Franchise: Terminator
Cast, crew, or production detail: Motion Capture Performance, Reboot, Same Actor, Multiple Roles, Same Role, Multiple Actors, Visual Effects
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR
Plot point: Artificial Intelligence, Robot, Time Travel, War
Social setting: End of the World, Post Apocalypse
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Skydance Productions, Paramount Pictures
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

When John Connor, leader of the human resistance, sends Sgt. Kyle Reese back to 1984 to protect Sarah Connor and safeguard the future, an unexpected turn of events creates a fractured timeline. Now, Sgt. Reese finds himself in a new and unfamiliar version of the past, where he is faced with unlikely allies, including the Guardian, dangerous new enemies, and an unexpected new mission: To reset the future…

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.
